name: Castillo de Montizón por la Torre de la Higuera — circular desde la Torre de Jan Abad
region_intro: Jogging routes around Torre De Juan Abad offer a variety of landscapes, from open countryside to paths near reservoirs. The region features gently rolling terrain, with some routes including moderate ascents. These running trails provide opportunities to explore the natural environment of this area. Best jogging routes around Torre De Juan Abad The most popular jogging route is Castillo de Montizón por la Torre de la Higuera — circular desde la Torre de Jan Abad, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This route features a moderate difficulty and includes some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is La Cabezuela Reservoir loop from Cerro del Manzano, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.6 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Local runners also love the Puerto de las Virtudes loop from Las Virtudes, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with a notable ascent, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Jogging around Torre De Juan Abad is defined by open fields, reservoir views, and paths with moderate elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter runs to more challenging distances. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 9 runners have used komoot to explore Torre De Juan Abad's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Torre De Juan Abad?
A: The running trails in Torre De Juan Abad feature a variety of landscapes, including open countryside and paths near reservoirs. You'll encounter gently rolling terrain, with some routes including moderate ascents, offering a diverse experience for runners.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Torre De Juan Abad?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, the area offers moderate trails that can be suitable for beginners looking for a bit of a challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Puebla del Príncipe&lt;/a&gt; is the shortest at just over 8 km and has moderate elevation changes.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for jogging routes in this region?
A: Running routes around Torre De Juan Abad vary in length and duration. You can find trails ranging from approximately 8 km to over 18 km. Depending on the route, completion times can range from about 1 hour to nearly 2 hours, catering to different fitness levels and time commitments.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Notable circular options include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211382343/castillo-de-montizon-por-la-torre-de-la-higuera-circular-desde-la-torre-de-jan-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montizón por la Torre de la Higuera — circular desde la Torre de Jan Abad&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabezuela Reservoir loop from Cerro del Manzano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes with views of reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region features routes that offer scenic views of reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabezuela Reservoir loop from Cerro del Manzano&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing excellent vistas of the reservoir and its natural surroundings.

Q: What can I see or explore along the jogging routes near Torre De Juan Abad?
A: While jogging, you might encounter various points of interest. Near some routes, you can find landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6658045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bullring (Las Virtudes)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6661524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5485461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest Area and Viewpoint of the Mari Sánchez Reservoir (La Cabezuela)&lt;/a&gt; also offers a great spot to pause and enjoy the scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Torre De Juan Abad?
A: The best time for running in Torre De Juan Abad generally depends on personal preference regarding temperature. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures, which are often ideal for longer runs. Summer can be quite warm, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended, while winter provides cooler, crisp conditions.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, experienced runners looking for a challenge will find options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabezuela Reservoir loop from Cerro del Manzano&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, spanning over 18 km with significant elevation changes, making it suitable for those seeking a more strenuous workout.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Torre De Juan Abad area?
A: There are currently 4 curated running routes available in the Torre De Juan Abad area, offering a range of moderate to difficult options for exploring the local landscape.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Torre De Juan Abad?
A: Runners who have explored the trails in Torre De Juan Abad often praise the varied terrain, including open fields and reservoir views, and the opportunities to experience the natural environment. The routes are appreciated for their moderate elevation changes, providing a good workout while enjoying the scenery.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass through or near historical sites?
A: While the routes primarily focus on natural landscapes, some trails may bring you close to historical or cultural points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211382343/castillo-de-montizon-por-la-torre-de-la-higuera-circular-desde-la-torre-de-jan-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montizón por la Torre de la Higuera — circular desde la Torre de Jan Abad&lt;/a&gt; route hints at historical elements in its name, suggesting proximity to the Montizón Castle area.
tips: Heading towards the Arroyo de la Vega, a tributary of the Río Gadalen, you can see this area before the first foothills of the Sierra Morena, in impressive landscapes!
As you pass along this road and on the right you can see on the nearest hill what is called Torreón de la Higuera, with the predominant tower above the rest.

Montizón Castle is a historic fortification located in the municipality of Villamanrique, in the province of Ciudad Real. Built in the 13th century, this castle belonged to the Order of Santiago and was the residence of the poet Jorge Manrique.
The castle is located in a strategic position above the Guadalén River, taking advantage of a natural cut in the hill where it is located. The keep, one of its most outstanding structures, is built on solid rock and offers impressive views of the surroundings.
Currently, the castle is privately owned and can be visited at specific times, generally on weekends and by appointment.
